pi_items.source: Verified via live environment SSH: file exists at this path, 53435 bytes, owned by ga:ga. Provenance: downloaded from https://github.com/opentoonz/opentoonz_sample (official OpenToonz sample repo) during setup_opentoonz.sh. Confirmed in GitHub API that dwanko_run.tnz exists at repo root (53435 bytes). The file is ASCII XML (TNZ format) with framecount=72, cameraRes=1920x1080, fps=24, generated by OpenToonz 1.2.0. Contains 10 levels: 01_sky, 02_trees, 03_ground, 04_bush1, 05_bush2, 06_bush3, 07_bush4, A_painted (character animation), shadow, raylit_source.

Verified via live environment SSH: file exists at this path, 330536 bytes (323 KB), owned by ga:ga. Downloaded from https://www.ghibli.jp/gallery/chihiro050.jpg (first URL in setup_task.sh array). File is JPEG, 1920x1038 pixels, RGB mode, created with Adobe Photoshop Elements 10.0 (EXIF metadata confirms real production artwork). Mean pixel value 88.0, std 57.5. Note: resolution is 1920x1038 (not 1080) - this is the native Ghibli gallery resolution for this image.

Verified via live environment SSH: directory exists, owned by ga:ga, permissions drwxrwxr-x, empty after setup_task.sh cleanup. Created by setup_task.sh line 11. Both export_result.sh and verifier.py reference this same path. Agent user 'ga' has write permissions (confirmed by touch test).

This is the task's REQUIRED output resolution, not the scene's native resolution. The dwanko_run.tnz native cameraRes is 1920x1080 (verified from TNZ XML). The agent must change the output resolution to 1280x720 in OpenToonz Output Settings. The value 1280 is consistent with the task description ('Set output resolution to 1280x720'). The export_result.sh checks img_width against rendered output.
Same as expected_width: this is the required output resolution. Native scene resolution is 1920x1080. Agent must change to 720 height. Consistent with task description and export_result.sh analysis.

The dwanko_run.tnz scene has framecount=72 (verified from TNZ XML header). The task asks the agent to 'render all frames', so successful completion would produce 72 frames. The minimum threshold of 20 is a generous lower bound to allow partial credit. This is consistent with other OpenToonz tasks (e.g., background_composite_render also uses min_frame_count=20 for the same 72-frame scene).

This is a task requirement, not a data property. The task description states 'Apply a Blur effect (value >= 8)'. OpenToonz Blur effect supports values from 0 to 100+. Value 8 is achievable. Confirmed Blur effect exists in the OpenToonz installation via live test: found BlurIno.pdf documentation and shader-based blur effects at /snap/opentoonz/147/share/opentoonz/stuff/. The standard Blur effect is available in FX Browser under 'Blur' category.

For 20+ PNG frames at 1280x720, each frame is typically 50-200KB depending on content complexity. 20 frames x 100KB average = 2000KB, far exceeding the 100KB threshold. The threshold is deliberately low to detect only completely failed renders (e.g., 0 bytes or blank images). Consistent with the export_result.sh total_size_kb measurement.

Verified via HTTP HEAD request: returns 200 OK, content-type image/jpeg, content-length 330536, server Apache via CloudFront, last-modified Thu 01 Oct 2020. Downloaded and inspected: JPEG 1920x1038 RGB, EXIF shows Adobe Photoshop Elements 10.0 Windows, datetime 2020:10:01. This is a real Studio Ghibli production background from the movie 'Spirited Away' (Sen to Chihiro no Kamikakushi), depicting the bathhouse area. The Ghibli gallery (ghibli.jp/gallery/) was opened to the public in September 2020 for free non-commercial use.

privileged_info_summary: The source scene is dwanko_run.tnz from the official OpenToonz sample repository (github.com/opentoonz/opentoonz_sample), located at /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz. It is an XML-based scene file (53435 bytes) containing 72 frames at 24 fps with a native camera resolution of 1920x1080. The scene has 10 levels including background layers (01_sky through 07_bush4), a character animation level (A_painted), a shadow level, and a raylit_source level. The background painting is at /home/ga/OpenToonz/backgrounds/night_city.jpg, downloaded from the official Studio Ghibli gallery (https://www.ghibli.jp/gallery/chihiro050.jpg). It is a JPEG image of 1920x1038 pixels (note: NOT 1080 - the Ghibli gallery image has slightly non-standard height), 330536 bytes, depicting a Spirited Away production background. The agent must: (1) open the scene, (2) import this background as a new Xsheet column placed behind the character, (3) expose the background across all 72 frames, (4) apply a Blur FX with value >= 8 to the background column ONLY (not the character columns) via the FX Schematic, (5) change the output resolution from native 1920x1080 to 1280x720, set format to PNG, and render all frames to /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/night_composite/. The output directory exists and is writable by user 'ga'. The export_result.sh script collects frame count, dimensions, timestamp validity, Laplacian variance for blur detection, and corner vs center brightness for composite verification, writing results to /tmp/task_result.json.
